<!-- Generated by /make-a-plan вҖ” technical architecture and contracts for agent implementation -->

# Design: Lecturer Guide вҖ” Interactive Cheat Sheet

## Overview

A single-page React application that renders the cybersecurity course lecturer's guide as an interactive, hierarchical reference tool. The app uses a sidebar for module-level navigation, recursive collapsible sections for content drill-down, and next/previous buttons for linear flow. Content lives in structured JS data files, completely separated from UI components.

Stack: React + Vite + Tailwind CSS. No router, no state management library вҖ” React state is sufficient for this scope.

## Architecture

```
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ        ModuleView             в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Sidebar  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Module 3 в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (points/examples в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ ...      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  /transition)    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ CollapsibleSectвҖҰ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ (nested child)   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  NavigationControls    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Data flow:**
1. `App` holds state: `activeModuleId`, `activeSectionId`, `expandedSections` (Set)
2. `Sidebar` reads `activeModuleId`, dispatches `onModuleSelect`
3. `ModuleView` reads the data file for the active module, renders `CollapsibleSection` tree
4. `CollapsibleSection` reads/writes `expandedSections` for its own ID
5. `NavigationControls` reads `activeSectionId`, walks the linear order array to find prev/next

## Components and Interfaces

### App

```typescript
// Top-level state
interface AppState {
  activeModuleId: string        // e.g., "intro", "module1", "day1-summary"
  activeSectionId: string       // e.g., "1.1", "2.3" вҖ” used by NavigationControls
  expandedSections: Set<string> // tracks which sections are expanded
}
```

**Responsibility:** Owns application state. Renders Sidebar + ModuleView + NavigationControls in a flex layout (sidebar fixed-width left, content area fills remaining space).

### Sidebar

```typescript
interface SidebarProps {
  modules: SidebarItem[]        // ordered list of navigable modules
  activeModuleId: string
  onModuleSelect: (moduleId: string) => void
}

interface SidebarItem {
  id: string                    // e.g., "module1"
  title: string                 // e.g., "РңРҫРҙСғР» 1: Р’СҠРІРөРҙРөРҪРёРө"
  shortTitle: string            // e.g., "1. Р’СҠРІРөРҙРөРҪРёРө" вҖ” for compact display
}
```

**Responsibility:** Renders the module list. Highlights active module. Calls `onModuleSelect` on click.

### CollapsibleSection

```typescript
interface CollapsibleSectionProps {
  section: Section              // the data for this section
  depth: number                 // nesting level (0 = top-level within module)
  isExpanded: boolean
  onToggle: (sectionId: string) => void
  expandedSections: Set<string>
}
```

**Responsibility:** Renders a section header (clickable) and body (animated expand/collapse). Recursively renders child `CollapsibleSection` components for nested subsections. Passes `expandedSections` down so children can read their own state.

### ContentBlock

```typescript
interface ContentBlockProps {
  type: "talking-points" | "examples" | "transition" | "time-marker" | "qa"
  content: string | string[]    // single string or bullet list
}
```

**Responsibility:** Renders content with the appropriate visual style based on type. Each type has a distinct left-border color, optional icon, and text styling.

### NavigationControls

```typescript
interface NavigationControlsProps {
  currentSectionId: string
  navigationOrder: string[]     // flat array of all section IDs in course order
  onNavigate: (sectionId: string) => void
}
```

**Responsibility:** Shows prev/next buttons. Determines prev/next section from `navigationOrder`. Calls `onNavigate` which triggers module switch + section expand + scroll.

## Data Models

### Module data structure

Each module is exported from its own file as a `CourseModule` object:

```typescript
interface CourseModule {
  id: string                    // "intro", "module1", "day1-summary", etc.
  title: string                 // "РңРҫРҙСғР» 1: Р’СҠРІРөРҙРөРҪРёРө РІ РәРёРұРөСҖСҒРёРіСғСҖРҪРҫСҒСӮСӮР°"
  duration: string              // "50 РјРёРҪ"
  timeMarker: string            // "Р”РөРҪ 1, 09:40 вҖ“ 10:30"
  note: string                  // "РўРҫРІР° Рө РІСҒСӮСҠРҝРёСӮРөР»РҪРёСҸСӮ РјРҫРҙСғР» вҖ” Р·Р°РҙР°Р№ СӮРҫРҪ, РұСҠРҙРё РөРҪРөСҖРіРёСҮРөРҪ."
  sections: Section[]
}

interface Section {
  id: string                    // "1.1", "1.2", "intro-main", etc.
  title: string                 // "РҡР°РәРІРҫ Рө РәРёРұРөСҖСҒРёРіСғСҖРҪРҫСҒСӮ вҖ” РҫСҒРҪРҫРІРҪРё РҝРҫРҪСҸСӮРёСҸ"
  talkingPoints: TalkingPoint[] // bullet list of what to say
  examples: string[]            // real-world examples
  transition: string            // "РҹР Р•РҘРһР” вҶ’" text
  children?: Section[]          // nested subsections (for deeper drill-down in future)
}

interface TalkingPoint {
  text: string                  // the point itself
  children?: TalkingPoint[]     // nested sub-bullets
}
```

### Navigation order

A single array exported from a navigation config file, defining the linear path through the entire course:

```typescript
const navigationOrder: string[] = [
  "intro-main",
  "1.1", "1.2", "1.3", "1.4",
  "2.1", "2.2", "2.3", "2.4",
  "3.1", "3.2", "3.3", "3.4",
  "4.1", "4.2", "4.3", "4.4", "4.5",
  "5.1",
  "day1-summary",
  "day2-review",
  "5.2", "5.3", "5.4",
  "6.1", "6.2", "6.3", "6.4"
]
```

### Section-to-module mapping

A lookup object mapping each section ID to its parent module ID, so NavigationControls can trigger module switches:

```typescript
const sectionToModule: Record<string, string> = {
  "intro-main": "intro",
  "1.1": "module1",
  "1.2": "module1",
  // ...
}
```

## Visual Design

### Content type color coding

| Content Type | Left Border | Icon | Text Style |
|---|---|---|---|
| Talking points | Blue (`blue-500`) | рҹ’¬ or bullet | Normal weight |
| Examples | Amber (`amber-500`) | рҹ’Ў | Italic |
| Transition | Green (`green-500`) | вҶ’ | Semi-bold, distinct background |
| Time marker | Purple (`purple-500`) | вҸұ | Badge/pill style |
| Q&A | Slate (`slate-400`) | вқ“ | Muted |

### Depth indentation

Each nesting level adds `ml-4` (16px) indent + progressively lighter left-border opacity:
- Depth 0: full opacity border
- Depth 1: 75% opacity
- Depth 2: 50% opacity
- Depth 3+: 40% opacity

### Collapse animation

CSS transition on `max-height` + `opacity`, 200-300ms ease. Use a wrapper div with `overflow-hidden` and transition between `max-height: 0` and the content's scroll height.

## Error Handling

Not applicable вҖ” this is a static, local-only reference tool with no external services, user input, or data persistence. If a data file has a structural issue, React's default error boundary will surface it during development.

## Testing Strategy

- Manual testing only for v1 (per PRD decision)
- Verification checklist: run `npm run dev`, click through all modules, verify content completeness against source Markdown, test next/prev navigation end-to-end
- No automated tests вҖ” the output is visual, and the tool is single-user
